steps.title: Identify the parenthetical phrase
steps.content: The phrase “claims ______ Valerie Trouet” interrupts the main clause, set off by commas on both sides. The blank should contain a word that identifies who Valerie Trouet is.

steps.title: Apply the rule for appositive nouns
steps.content: When one noun identifies or renames another (an appositive), no punctuation separates them. “Dendrochronologist Valerie Trouet” directly identifies her profession with no punctuation between the two noun elements. Choices A, B, and D each add punctuation (dash, comma, colon) that would incorrectly split the appositive. That matches choice C — C is correct.
